mentions.note: Cramer gets up 2 a.m. for conference calls; magnificent company. Not owned for Travel Trust because Nvidia (largest client) also owned and no need for both. Taiwan Semi very good but Taiwan risk similar to Nvidia.

Caller Craig calls it amazing tech/manufacturing company addressing energy via 2nm+ chips; Cramer gets up 2am for earnings call, acknowledges magnificent company; owns Nvidia instead (largest TSMC client) for Chapel Trust—no need for redundant Taiwan exposure.
Manufacturing partner for AMD and Nvidia; should deliver monster quarter; could help shake off Nvidia sellers.
Reported one of strongest quarters ever with insatiable AI chip demand; spending $50B+ to keep up with orders; validates AI investment thesis and hyperscaler profitability.
Amazing semiconductor manufacturer; critical for Nvidia and others; excellent tech holding.
Partners with Nvidia on high-end chips; no bottleneck or shortage in their segment; critical to global AI infrastructure.
Semiconductor manufacturer; key partner to Nvidia; critical to high-end chip production.
Blowout quarter; guiding 52-56B capex for 2026 (up 27-37%) vs. <45B expected; management says supply-demand won't balance until 2028-2029; bullish for years of equipment orders.
Cramer likes Taiwan Semi but prefers Nvidia; both solid but NVDA is the pick on weakness.
Nvidia's chip manufacturer; reported monster quarter; CEO C.C. Wei signals shift to agentic AI command-action mode; validates Nvidia's continued dominance.
Terrific situation; very good. Stock went down on perfectly good quarter last week; liked that quarter. Room to grow.
Supply-constrained on massive demand; customers cannot source chips elsewhere; guaranteed demand dynamic with pricing power.
Chip manufacturer essential to AI infrastructure layer. Global capacity constraints driving adoption of optical interconnects.
Indispensable chokepoint in advanced semiconductor manufacturing; may not skyrocket like peers but strong fundamentals. 'Think the world of it, going to go higher.'
